commands.review_summary:
The PR forwards six existing canonical media metadata fields into plugin `message_received` event metadata and adds focused mapper regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +24. Total +30 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main derives media metadata and exposes it on `inbound_claim`, while `toPluginMessageReceivedEvent` drops those fields before the WhatsApp hook runner receives the event.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer handling is needed because automation cannot supply the contributor's real WhatsApp/plugin behavior proof, and checks still need to pass.

target.title: fix(hooks): preserve media metadata on message_received (Fixes #87229)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87229.

Preserve inbound media metadata when emitting plugin `message_received` hooks.

The canonical inbound hook context already carries `mediaPath`, `mediaUrl`, `mediaType`, and the multi-attachment arrays, and `inbound_claim` was already forwarding those fields. `message_received` was the inconsistent path: it emitted the hook but dropped the media metadata from `event.metadata`.

This patch keeps the change narrow:

- forward the existing media metadata fields in `toPluginMessageReceivedEvent`
- add a regression test that covers multi-attachment WhatsApp-style payloads

It does not change media download behavior, hook routing, or the canonical inbound context derivation.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:

- plugin `message_received` hooks now receive the same inbound media metadata that was already available on the canonical context and already exposed on `inbound_claim`

Environment tested:

- local checkout of current `upstream/main`
- focused hook-mapper test path through the production mapper entrypoints

Exact command run after the patch:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts`

Evidence after the fix:

- the new regression builds a canonical inbound message with `MediaPaths`, `MediaUrls`, and `MediaTypes`
- `toPluginMessageReceivedEvent(canonical)` now preserves:
  - `metadata.mediaPath`
  - `metadata.mediaUrl`
  - `metadata.mediaType`
  - `metadata.mediaPaths`
  - `metadata.mediaUrls`
  - `metadata.mediaTypes`

Observed result:

- the focused mapper suite passed with the new assertion covering the previously dropped fields

What was not tested:

- live WhatsApp gateway ingress or end-to-end plugin delivery in a running gateway session

Proof limitation:

- I also tried a standalone `npx tsx --eval` probe against the mapper entrypoint, but this fresh worktree does not currently resolve the local `@openclaw/fs-safe/config` dependency for that path. I kept the proof grounded in the repo test runner rather than claim a broader runtime repro I could not complete in this environment.

## Risk

Low.

This only forwards metadata fields that already exist on the canonical inbound context and already ship through the related `inbound_claim` event. The change does not alter message routing, authorization, or media staging behavior.
target.author: deepujain
